# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1035
- **Lines of code**: 347,467
- **Languages**: TypeScript (875), CSS (44), Markdown (32), JavaScript (31), JSON (30)
- **Zones**: 35 (35 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-06T22:09:41.995Z

## Architecture Zones

### Autonomous Agent (`autonomous-agent`)

The hench package: autonomous task execution via agent loops, tool dispatch, brief generation, and run recording against the rex PRD.

- Files: 159
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/autonomous-agent/context.md`

### DOM Performance Monitoring (`dom-performance-monitoring`)

Encapsulates the DOM performance monitoring utility and its React hook abstraction for the viewer.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.67, Coupling: 0.33
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/dom-performance-monitoring/context.md`

Comprehensive CLI and architecture E2E tests covering orchestration, delegation, error handling, config, and web server behavior.

- Files: 14

- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/e2e-tests/context.md`

Root-level entry points and configuration that coordinate the three-package pipeline (sourcevision → rex → hench) without importing from any package directly.

- Files: 27

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- rex-gateway.ts in hench re-exports 8 functions from rex with no version-lock or compatibility smoke test; breaking changes to rex's public API will only surface at runtime inside an agent loop, making them expensive to diagnose — add a gateway compatibility test [warning] (autonomous-agent)
- Call graph reports coupling=0 while cross-zone import table records 1 outgoing import to web-viewer — metric disagreement between analysis passes produces unreliable zone health scores and must be resolved before coupling data can be trusted for this zone. [warning] (dom-performance-monitoring)
- Zone contains only 2 production files; the overhead of maintaining a separate zone boundary (including the disputed cross-zone import) outweighs the structural benefit — merge into web-viewer unless additional files are actively planned. [info] (dom-performance-monitoring)
- MCP HTTP transport (the recommended integration path) has no E2E test coverage; the suite tests CLI process boundaries but not the HTTP session lifecycle, leaving the primary MCP surface unvalidated at the process boundary level. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- No shared E2E fixture or helper module detected across 14 test files; duplicated process-spawn and environment setup logic increases maintenance burden and risks inconsistent test environments between files — extract common setup into a shared e2e-helpers module. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- architecture-policy.test.js encodes zone IDs and tier boundaries statically; zone renames or structural changes will not automatically invalidate the policy assertions, creating a category of silent false-passes — tie policy checks to the live zone graph output rather than hardcoded identifiers. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- CLI argument interfaces between orchestration scripts and domain package CLIs are untyped; any CLI signature change in rex, hench, or sourcevision is a silent breaking change with no compile-time or schema-level safety net — add contract tests or a shared CLI-args schema to make this boundary explicit [warning] (monorepo-orchestration)
- usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts depends on web-viewer (the UI application layer) from within a background service zone — scheduler lifecycle should be driven by an interface or event emitter, not a direct import of the viewer module, to prevent initialization-order coupling in tests and production startup [critical] (task-usage-tracking)
- No shared design-token layer exists between viewer-static-assets and web-landing despite both being presentation zones in the same package; brand drift between landing page and viewer is undetectable at build time [warning] (viewer-static-assets)
- BROWSER_ERROR_CODE_5.md and MEMORY_PROFILE.md are debugging artifacts committed to the web package root with no clear retention policy — they pollute the package root and should be moved to docs/ or removed [info] (web-build-infrastructure)
- ... and 5 more

### Patterns

- E2E suite has zero source-level coupling but a hidden build-time dependency on all packages; CI must enforce a build step before e2e execution. This should be documented or enforced via a pre-test script to prevent silent false-negatives when a package fails to compile. [warning] (e2e-tests)
- The runtime-state zone is a shared mutable sink readable by both rex and hench packages without creating import-graph coupling — this is an intentional design but concurrent write safety is implicit; documenting the write-access protocol would prevent future race conditions. [warning] (rex-runtime-state)
- Absence of an index/facade module means task-usage-tracking has no encapsulated public surface; consumers couple directly to internal service files, weakening the zone boundary. [warning] (task-usage-tracking)
- Build scripts (build.js, dev.js) in this zone operate at the package boundary but are grouped with UI components (elapsed-time.ts, task-audit.ts) — splitting into a pure build-config group and a reusable-components group would clarify which files are tooling versus production API surface. [warning] (web-build-infrastructure)
- Call graph ratio (6495 internal : 23 outgoing) confirms web-dashboard is a near-terminal hub — outgoing calls are exclusively routed through gateway files, validating the gateway pattern at the call level. [info] (web-dashboard)

### Relationships

- Hench is the only execution-layer package importing from a domain package (rex via gateway); if rex's public API changes, hench's gateway is the single choke-point — this is good design, but the gateway has no explicit version-lock or compatibility test to catch breaking changes early. [warning] (autonomous-agent)
- Cross-zone import direction 'dom → web-viewer' conflicts with documented leaf-node status; verify whether dom-performance-monitoring imports anything from web-viewer or whether the arrow direction in the import table denotes 'exports to'. If dom does import from web-viewer, this is a circular dependency that must be resolved. [critical] (dom-performance-monitoring)
- Orchestration layer's zero-coupling guarantee is enforced structurally but not contractually — CLI argument interfaces between cli.js and domain package CLIs are untyped; adding schema validation or contract tests would make the spawn boundary explicit. [warning] (monorepo-orchestration)
- viewer-static-assets has zero import-graph coupling but carries hidden deployment coupling to web-dashboard via build manifest filenames; this contract is not enforced by TypeScript and breaks silently if build output names change. [warning] (viewer-static-assets)
- boundary-check.test.ts appears to test zone-boundary contracts rather than websocket internals; relocating it to an integration test zone would remove the external coupling that degrades this zone's cohesion score. [warning] (websocket-infrastructure)
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 142 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (autonomous-agent)
- 159 files in a single zone suggests the internal structure is dense; sourcevision may benefit from per-subdirectory zone hints to surface finer-grained architectural boundaries within hench. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Agent analysis files (adaptive.ts, review.ts, spin.ts) represent domain-specific loop heuristics — documenting their interaction order would help contributors understand the agent's decision pipeline. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero external coupling confirm the rex-gateway pattern is working as intended for this package. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e-tests)
- 14 E2E test files with no shared fixtures module detected; if setup/teardown logic is duplicated across files, a shared helper could reduce maintenance burden. [info] (e2e-tests)
- Perfect cohesion and zero coupling are ideal for an E2E suite — these tests correctly treat the CLI as a black box. [info] (e2e-tests)
- architecture-policy.test.js should be reviewed periodically to ensure it encodes current tier boundaries; policy tests that are not updated alongside architectural changes become stale and misleading. [info] (e2e-tests)
- Bidirectional coupling: "task-usage-tracking" ↔ "web-dashboard" (1+3 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "web-build-infrastructure" ↔ "web-dashboard" (4+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- ... and 34 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "autonomous-agent" has files across 31 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Align the zone ID with the package name: rename 'autonomous-agent' to 'hench' (or 'hench-core') so that sourcevision zone health reports are directly mappable to package ownership, CLI commands (rex, hench, sourcevision), and directory paths without a mental translation step. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Run intra-package call-graph analysis on hench to detect any emerging circular call patterns between its internal subdirectories (agent/, prd/, tools/) before they compound — the rex circular call pattern (239+100 calls) was only found via call-graph analysis, not zone metrics, and hench's 2838 internal calls make it the most likely next site for a hidden cycle. [warning] (autonomous-agent)
- packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ contains files from 4 zones (autonomous-agent, dom-performance-monitoring, web-build-infrastructure, web-dashboard) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Add a dependency-cruiser or similar import-boundary rule to CI that enforces the gateway pattern: any cross-package import that does not pass through the designated gateway module should fail the build. This converts a trust-based convention into a machine-enforced constraint. [warning]
- Add a zone-type tag ('test' | 'production' | 'config' | 'data') to zone metadata and apply type-appropriate cohesion/coupling thresholds in health scoring — applying production-code thresholds to test zones (web-unit, e2e) and data zones (rex-runtime-state) produces category errors that make zone comparison reports misleading. [info]
- All micro-zones (< 5 production files) in the web package lack facade index modules, meaning their zone boundaries exist only in the zone detection metadata and are invisible to the TypeScript compiler — mandate an 'index.ts' barrel for every zone with 2+ production files to make zone membership compiler-visible and reduce the risk of zone boundaries eroding silently. [warning]
- Audit all zone IDs against their contained filenames for grammatical form consistency (noun vs gerund vs adjective) — dom-performance-monitoring/dom-performance-monitor is the only detected mismatch but the same inconsistency could exist in other zones if zone IDs were assigned without reference to the actual filenames. [info]
- Establish a documented testing convention for the utility+hook pattern: every hook wrapper (use-*.ts) should have a corresponding hook test file alongside the utility test — currently dom-performance-monitoring applies the production pattern but not the test pattern, and without a written convention future hooks will follow the same incomplete template. [warning]
- Establish a zone naming convention and document it in PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md: either all zones use their package directory name as a prefix (hench-core, rex-core, sourcevision-core) or all use pure role descriptors — the current mix of package-name zones and role-descriptor zones makes it impossible to infer zone ownership from the zone ID alone. [info]
- ... and 36 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
